name: Castillo de Alba de Aliste Trail
name_original: Castillo de Alba — circular por la orilla del río Aliste
description: Hike the moderate 4.9-mile (8.0 km) Castillo de Alba circular trail along the Río Aliste, featuring castle ruins and tranquil riverside path
region_intro: Hiking around Videmala offers routes through varied terrain, characterized by river valleys, waterfalls, and historical sites. The landscape features gentle elevations, with paths often following watercourses like the Río Duero. Hikers can expect a mix of natural beauty and cultural points of interest, providing diverse outdoor experiences. Best hiking trails around Videmala The most popular hiking route is Route of the Perones and Cadozos waterfall — circular from Ricobayo de Alba, a moderate 4.8 miles (7.8 km) trail that takes 2 hours 10 minutes to complete. This circular route features scenic waterfalls and river views. Another top favourite among local hikers is La Palia Waterfall - round trip from Valdoradas bridge, an easy 2.1 miles (3.4 km) path. This shorter route offers a direct experience of a local waterfall. Local hikers also love the Castillo de Alba de Aliste Trail, a 4.9 miles (7.9 km) trail leading through historical landscapes, often completed in about 2 hours 13 minutes. Hiking around Videmala is defined by river paths, waterfalls, and historical points of interest. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to more challenging routes. The routes in Videmala are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from a few reviews. More than 20 hikers have used komoot to explore Videmala's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Videmala?
A: There are 9 distinct hiking trails around Videmala, offering a variety of experiences. These include 3 easy routes, 5 moderate routes, and 1 difficult trail, catering to different fitness levels and preferences.

Q: Are there any easy, family-friendly walks in Videmala?
A: Yes, Videmala offers several easy routes suitable for families. A great option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1026734793/la-palia-waterfall-round-trip-from-valdoradas-bridge" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Palia Waterfall - round trip from Valdoradas bridge&lt;/a&gt;, an easy 2.1-mile (3.4 km) path that provides a direct experience of a local waterfall. Another easy choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1283120270/rio-duero-desde-pino-del-oro-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Río Duero desde Pino del Oro — circular&lt;/a&gt;, which is 3.4 miles (5.4 km) long.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes in Videmala?
A: Yes, several circular routes are available. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1026677750/route-of-the-perones-and-cadozos-waterfall-circular-from-ricobayo-de-alba" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Route of the Perones and Cadozos waterfall — circular from Ricobayo de Alba&lt;/a&gt; is a popular moderate option, covering 4.8 miles (7.8 km) and featuring scenic waterfalls. Another circular route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1283120270/rio-duero-desde-pino-del-oro-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Río Duero desde Pino del Oro — circular&lt;/a&gt;, an easy 3.4-mile (5.4 km) trail.

Q: What kind of natural features or landmarks can I expect to see on the trails?
A: The trails around Videmala are characterized by river valleys, waterfalls, and historical sites. You can expect to see impressive waterfalls like those on the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1026677750/route-of-the-perones-and-cadozos-waterfall-circular-from-ricobayo-de-alba" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Route of the Perones and Cadozos waterfall&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1026734793/la-palia-waterfall-round-trip-from-valdoradas-bridge" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Palia Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;. The landscape also features the Río Duero, which many paths follow. Additionally, you might encounter historical points of interest such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5714203" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of San Pedro de la Nave&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5459672" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ricobayo Dam&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any historical trails or routes with cultural significance?
A: Yes, the region offers trails that pass through historical landscapes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1282989725/castillo-de-alba-de-aliste-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Alba de Aliste Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a notable example, leading you through areas with historical importance. You can also find landmarks like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5714203" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of San Pedro de la Nave&lt;/a&gt; and various bridges, such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3409877" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Requejo Bridge&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5442569" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valdoradas Bridge&lt;/a&gt;, which add to the cultural experience.

Q: What is the best season for hiking in Videmala?
A: Videmala's climate is generally Mediterranean with continental influences, meaning hot, dry summers and mild, wet winters. Spring and autumn are often ideal for hiking, offering pleasant temperatures and vibrant natural scenery. Summers can be very warm, so early morning or late afternoon hikes are recommended, while winters are generally mild enough for hiking, though some paths might be wet.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Videmala?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars. Reviewers often praise the diverse terrain, the scenic river paths, and the beautiful waterfalls. The mix of natural beauty and historical points of interest also contributes to a highly enjoyable hiking experience.

Q: Are there any longer or more challenging hikes for experienced hikers?
A: For experienced hikers seeking a challenge, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1123665143/puente-de-valdelasmozas-from-el-campillo-circular-via-almendra-and-andavias" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puente de Valdelasmozas from El Campillo – circular via Almendra and Andavías&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult route. This extensive trail covers 15.7 miles (25.3 km) and typically takes around 6 hours and 44 minutes to complete, offering significant elevation changes and a rewarding experience.

Q: What should I bring for a hike in Videmala?
A: Given the varied terrain and potential for different weather conditions, it's advisable to bring appropriate hiking footwear, water, snacks, and sun protection. Depending on the season, layers of clothing are recommended. Always check the local weather forecast before heading out. For longer routes, a map or GPS device is also beneficial.

Q: Are there any less crowded hiking trails in Videmala?
A: While the region is generally not heavily trafficked, for a quieter experience, consider exploring some of the less-known local paths away from the most popular waterfall routes. Trails that follow the Río Duero, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1283120270/rio-duero-desde-pino-del-oro-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Río Duero desde Pino del Oro — circular&lt;/a&gt;, can offer peaceful stretches, especially during off-peak hours or weekdays.

Q: Are the hiking trails in Videmala dog-friendly?
A: Many trails in Videmala are suitable for dogs, especially those that traverse natural landscapes and river paths. However, it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially in areas with wildlife or near livestock, and to carry water for both yourself and your pet. Always check local regulations for specific trails or protected areas.

Q: Where can I find parking for the trailheads?
A: Parking availability varies by trailhead. For routes starting in villages like Ricobayo de Alba or near Valdoradas bridge, you can often find designated parking areas or roadside parking. It's best to check the specific route details on komoot for information on parking facilities near your chosen starting point.
tips: Medieval fortress located on a defensive hill. The ruined castle offers spectacular views of the Aliste River canyon. The first references to the castle date back to the 10th century.

faqs.question: How long does it take to hike the Castillo de Alba — circular by the bank of the Río Aliste trail?
faqs.answer: This circular hike typically takes around 2 hours and 13 minutes to complete. This duration is an estimate and can vary based on your pace and how often you stop to enjoy the scenery.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this trail?
faqs.answer: The trail is considered to be of moderate difficulty. It's generally accessible and intuitive, making it suitable for most hikers, including families, though it's not recommended for strollers due to varied terrain.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trail?
faqs.answer: You can start this circular trail from the town of Castillo de Alba, where parking is available. This is a convenient starting point for your hike.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain will I encounter on the hike?
faqs.answer: You'll experience a varied rural setting, primarily walking along the banks of the Río Aliste. The path includes natural environments, and you might encounter traditional cultural elements like old animal shoeing posts (potros de herrar). The sound of streams adds to the tranquil atmosphere.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: A significant highlight is the historic Castle of Alba de Aliste, a medieval fortress offering impressive views despite being in ruins. You'll also enjoy spectacular views of the Aliste River and, in some sections, the Esla River reservoir, all while following the calming Río Aliste.

faqs.question: When is the best time of year to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: While the trail can be enjoyed year-round, spring and autumn often offer the most pleasant weather for hiking in the Zamora province, with comfortable temperatures and vibrant natural scenery. Always check local weather conditions before you go.

faqs.question: Is the Castillo de Alba — circular by the bank of the Río Aliste trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: The trail is generally dog-friendly, allowing you to enjoy the scenic walk with your canine companion. However, always ensure your dog is under control, preferably on a leash, and remember to clean up after them to preserve the natural environment for everyone.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or have to pay entrance fees for this hike?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to hike this trail. It is freely accessible to the public.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities or places to rest along the way?
faqs.answer: Yes, along the route, particularly after crossing a bridge, you'll find areas equipped with tables and benches. These spots are ideal for taking a break, having a snack, and enjoying the surrounding landscape.

faqs.question: Does this trail connect to any other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the region is historically significant, and this trail connects to the Camino Portugués of the Vía de la Plata, which is a route of the Camino de Santiago.

faqs.question: What is the elevation gain and loss on this hike?
faqs.answer: The trail features an elevation gain of approximately 155 meters (509 feet) and an elevation loss of about 152 meters (499 feet), contributing to its moderate difficulty rating.
